LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS GREGORY HAMEL AND ERSY SHIVERS' MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES AND LITIGATION EXPENSES. THE COURT GRANTS $220,000 TO PLAINTIFFS AS REASONABLE ATTORNEY'S FEES. THE COURT SLIGHTLY REDUCES THE CLAIMED FEES BECAUSE SEVERAL OF THE MORGAN/MARSH BILLING ENTRIES ARE VAGUE BLOCK BILLS THAT OBSCURE THE NATURE OF THE WORK PERFORMED. THE COURT DOES NOT AWARD PLAINTIFF ITS PARKING AND MILEAGE LITIGATION COSTS. THE OTHER CLAIMED FEES ARE REASONABLE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-23-2014 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Sep-18-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - GREGORY HAMEL, TEL. NO. 621-7475, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN COURT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - NOTICE WAS NOT GIVEN TO COUNSEL AFTER TENTATIVE RULING CAME OUT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE LAW FIRM OF MORGAN, FRANICH, FREDKIN & MARSH'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL FOR THE PLAINTIFFS GREGORY HAMEL and ERSY SHIVERS IS Granted. No opposition filed. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976

MOTION To Tax Costs
Off Calendar Jul-17-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Stephen V. Myers, Esq. of the Law Office of John A. Biard, Tel. 925.945.4435, for defendant and moving party, via Court Call;
Anthony Marsh, Esq., of Morgan Franich Fredkin & Marsh, Tel. 408.288.8288 ext.117, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT 360 6TH STREET OWNERS ASSOCIATION'S Motion To Tax Costs is granted in part. The court grants the motion as to category 4 [expert deposition fees]. CCP 1033.5(b)(1) provides that only court ordered expert fees are recoverable. Bussey v. Affleck (1990) 225 Cal.App.3d 1162, is not persuasive authority to the contrary. See Robert L. Cloud & Assoc., Inc. v. Mikesell (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 1141, 1154. The court grants as to category 13 [mileage] because the costs of going to and from court are not recoverable. See Ladas v. California State Auto. Assn. (1993) 19 Cal.App.4th 761, 775. The court denies as to the remaining costs. Those costs (mediation and messenger fees) were reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976

Ntc Of Mtn For Attorneys' Fees And Litigation Expenses
Off Calendar Jul-17-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Anthony Marsh, Esq., of Morgan Franich Fredkin & Marsh, Tel. 408.288.8288 ext.117, for plaintiffs and moving party;
Stephen V. Myers, Esq. of the Law Office of John A. Biard, Tel. 925.945.4435, for defendant and opposing party, via Court Call.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on PLAINTIFFS GREGORY HAMEL and ERSY SHIVERS' Motion For Attorneys' Fees And Litigation Expenses. The court grants $220,000 to plaintiffs as reasonable attorney's fees. The court slightly reduces the claimed fees because several of the Morgan/Marsh billing entries are vague block bills that obscure the nature of the work performed. The court does not award plaintiff its parking and mileage litigation costs. The other claimed fees are reasonable. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976

MOTION To Tax Costs
Off Calendar Jul-10-2014 Continued To Jul-17-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT 360 6TH STREET OWNERS ASSOCIATION's Motion To Tax Costs is continued to July 17, 2014 to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than July 10, 2014 at 4:00 p.m. or the motion will be denied.
